config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar yang dilindungi, karena tanpa itu masyarakat mudah tergelincir menjadi menekan orang yang lemah atas nama “kepentingan bersama”. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat pembatasan yang sangat jelas dan proporsional, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dasar seenaknya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak individu dihormati dan kepentingan umum dijaga bersamaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over individual rights. From an American perspective, I am wary of any idea that lets “the common good” become a blank check for the state or the majority to override speech, privacy, due process, or minority protections. I do not think rights are absolute in every situation, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully, narrowly, and with real safeguards. When society’s interests are treated as more important by default, it becomes too easy to excuse abuse in the name of order or efficiency.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya dijaga adalah keseimbangan yang ketat dan akuntabel. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih kuat kalau hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, dan perlakuan adil tidak mudah dikorbankan. Menurut saya, prioritas boleh bergeser dalam keadaan darurat yang benar-benar jelas, namun itu harus sementara, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how governments and majorities justify overreach. From an American perspective, I see a real danger in letting vague claims of the "common good" override speech, privacy, due process, or minority rights, especially since those protections are supposed to limit power when it is most tempting to expand it. That said, I do think society can restrict individual freedom in narrow cases like public safety or real emergencies, but only with clear limits, evidence, and accountability. Without that restraint, "society first" too easily becomes a slogan that excuses abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena, dalam pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk menekan kritik dan membenarkan pembatasan yang semestinya tidak perlu. Hak individu bukan lawan dari kepentingan sosial; justru hak seperti kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, dan perlakuan adil adalah pagar agar “kepentingan umum” tidak berubah jadi alat mayoritas atau negara untuk semena-mena. Saya setuju bahwa dalam keadaan darurat yang nyata prioritas bisa bergeser, tetapi harus sangat terbatas, berbasis bukti, dan diawasi ketat. Tanpa itu, slogan “masyarakat di atas individu” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran penyalahgunaan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a default rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily that logic can be used to justify censorship, surveillance, and abuse of minority groups. I do think there are narrow cases where society can temporarily limit certain freedoms for public safety or a real emergency, but those limits should be tightly bounded, evidence-based, and subject to accountability. What I reject is the idea that “the common good” is automatically more important, because once that becomes the norm, individual rights stop being real protections and become permissions the state can revoke whenever it wants. A healthy society is built by protecting rights first and then making exceptions only when absolutely necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
